title: Typical Lilim Weaponry or Guns Used in Eva
display_title: Typical Lilim Weaponry or Guns Used in Eva
wikitext:
[]: Typical Lilim Weaponry (or Guns Used in Eva)             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Sat Aug 28, 2010 8:21 am I stumbled upon this site while checking as to what weapons were used in some of my favorite films. So far I'm impressed with the research, and they got the nomenclature right: http://www.imfdb.org/index.php/Neon_Genesis_Evangelion Discuss. I find this very useful for fanfic writing (except for some author's liberties). :)

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Sat Aug 28, 2010 10:03 am It looks like who ever made this put a large amount of effort and research into this. Now if only they could have done the same for the Eva's weaponry.

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Sat Aug 28, 2010 1:44 pm Basically they pause the vid, then zoom in to see what gun a character is holding, and sometimes they even manage to go down to the details and determine the exact model and make (Ritsuko's obscure Russian sidearm in EoE is one example).

[]:             Quote Post by thomson777 » Sun Sep 26, 2010 11:00 pm good job to whoever researched this it would be fun to see this go in the wiki at this site.

[]:             Quote Post by SAWgunner » Mon Sep 27, 2010 1:47 am Nice, I have the same piece as Misato.

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Mon Sep 27, 2010 1:50 pm Good find, still have to buy Misato's USP but they're like 800 - 900 dollars last time I checked.

[]:             Quote Post by SAWgunner » Mon Sep 27, 2010 2:27 pm Yeah, they're really expensive. If I didn't get an employee discount, I wouldn't have bought it.

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Mon Sep 27, 2010 6:58 pm They haven't took note of Rebuild, and what piece Kaji was packing there (probably a USP with a laser light module).

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Mon Sep 27, 2010 7:41 pm never realized gendo used a cz-75.

[]:             Quote Post by MugwumpHasNoLiver » Mon Sep 27, 2010 8:34 pm I'm just impressed that they managed to deduce what gun Gendo was using from the one angle that we see it from. And, actually, I'm even more impressed that that the design team actually drew different guns for everybody, with enough subtle attention to detail that somebody could deduce it from that angle.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Sep 27, 2010 9:02 pm MugwumpHasNoLiver wrote: I'm just impressed that they managed to deduce what gun Gendo was using from the one angle that we see it from. And, actually, I'm even more impressed that that the design team actually drew different guns for everybody, with enough subtle attention to detail that somebody could deduce it from that angle.

[]:             Quote Post by SAWgunner » Mon Sep 27, 2010 9:36 pm I agree, it definitely belongs there.

[]:             Quote Post by Cody MacArthur Fett » Mon Sep 27, 2010 10:28 pm A very fascinating website. I cross checked everything with Modern Firearms and there is not a single mis-designation. Definitely a resource for use later, and I am wholeheartedly in agreement that said intel should be uploaded to the Eva Geeks wiki as well.

[]:             Quote Post by ath » Tue Sep 28, 2010 9:33 am Hyper Shinchan wrote: I wonder if their work could be included directly in the evageek wiki with the proper credits.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Sep 28, 2010 10:23 am I very much doubt there's anything on that site that isn't free for use other than his exact wording, regardless of what the creator thinks.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Sep 28, 2010 2:02 pm I've already put up the page. I pulled images independently from wikipedia or various gun sites and took all the screenshots myself (a lot of their screnshots are from Manga's blurry release). I rewrote all the text myself. Anywhere where I use information that they've provided, I sourced them. There shouldn't be a problem.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Sep 28, 2010 4:16 pm looks like you improved on the flamethrower entry as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Cody MacArthur Fett » Tue Sep 28, 2010 6:44 pm Cool. I just took a look at the page and everything looks fairly well put together, however I did notice some inaccuracies with the G11 segment -- can't believe I didn't catch them before. The G11 was a prototype in the late 19 60s , and the G11 K2 was an actual production model originally fielded in 1989 of which 1000 were delivered to the Bundeswehr in 1990 before the program was promptly canceled by the German government. Also, the current entry comes a little too close to the original page's wording, might want to mix it up a bit more just to be safe.

[]: Re: Typical Lilim Weaponry (or Guns Used in Eva)             Quote Post by Assasinate » Mon Oct 18, 2010 6:00 am soul.assassin wrote: I stumbled upon this site while checking as to what weapons were used in some of my favorite films. So far I'm impressed with the research, and they got the nomenclature right: http://www.imfdb.org/index.php/Neon_Genesis_Evangelion Discuss. I find this very useful for fanfic writing (except for some author's liberties). :)

[]:             Quote Post by jota » Mon Oct 18, 2010 7:44 pm Very nice site indeed, of course it would be awesome if they have done something about the actual EVA weapons, but the effort of the crew of that page is incredible. I always believed that the Magnum on KZ2 was the colt anaconda, and in fact it is (: BTW the gun i found the most interesting is the Ritsuko uses in EoE, very funny one
